facade_detail.composition: The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its house-form, semi-detached typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 1, 1 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 1 principal entrance.

facade_description: Two-storey converted Victorian semi-detached house with a light orange brick upper facade and a pale blue awning over a narrow shopfront (Aion Amor Ltd). The facade has been simplified to a plain storefront-and-sign composition, with one centred upper window and narrow storefront glazing below. A single recessed commercial entry sits beneath the awning. Very little ornament remains beyond the brick box form. A flat roofline hides behind a parapet with a rooftop privacy screen. The neighbouring Sanagan's facade confirms the shared storefront rhythm along this stretch.
